title: When All Is Said
content:
When all is said
    And all is done
    Beneath the Sun,
    And Man lies dead;

    When all the earth
    Is a cold grave,
    And no more brave
    Bright things have birth;

    When cooling sun
    And stone-cold world,
    Together hurled,
    Flame up as one, 

    O Sons of Men,
    When all is flame,
    What of your fame
    And splendour then?

    When all is fire
    And flaming air,
    What of your rare
    And high desire

    To turn the clod
    To a thing divine,
    The earth a shrine,
    And Man the God?
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, J. D. C. Fellow delivers a powerful performance in "When All Is Said"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
